ops.title: bin/ab: remote-session cleanup + identity edge holes
ops.description: Pre-existing bin/ab holes surfaced in the abwc 0.11.0 round-2 review (not introduced by 0.11.0): (5) create_session POST can succeed while still in process-substitution before created_sid is armed → leak window; created_sid cleared just before the final exec → an exec failure/signal leaves the session live; ab close installs no signal traps. (10) outside a recognized Claude session cookiesync requestor falls back to parent PID (persistence defeated) and the slug sanitizer is non-injective (a/b and a?b both → ab-a-b, sharing browser state). All predate abwc 0.11.0. Consider: arm created_sid before the POST returns via a temp marker; trap signals in close; a collision-resistant slug (hash suffix).

ops.text: no create→arm window can leak a session
ab close releases even if interrupted mid-close
distinct requestors cannot collide onto one slug
